 AMAZON BESTSELLERS DATA ANALYSIS REPORT

 1. Introduction
This report presents an analysis of Amazon bestselling books dataset containing 550 records with attributes such as book title, author, user ratings, number of reviews, price, year, and genre.

The goal of this analysis is to extract meaningful insights about:
- Customer preferences  
- Pricing trends  
- Genre performance  
- Author dominance  
- Relationship between ratings and reviews  

---

2. Dataset Overview

| Column | Description |
|------|------------|
| Name | Title of the book |
| Author | Author of the book |
| User Rating | Rating given by users (out of 5) |
| Reviews | Number of customer reviews |
| Price | Price of the book |
| Year | Year of publication |
| Genre | Fiction or Non-Fiction |

 Key Observations:
- Total records: 550
- No missing values
- Data types are consistent and usable

---

3. Data Quality and Cleaning

- No null values detected
- Column names verified and standardized
- Data types appropriate for analysis

---

 4. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

 4.1 Genre Distribution
- Non-Fiction dominates the dataset

 4.2 User Ratings Analysis
- Most books are rated between 4.5 and 4.9
- Low-rated books rarely appear as bestsellers

4.3 Reviews Analysis
- Some books have over 20,000 reviews
- Review counts vary widely

4.4 Price Distribution
- Most books are priced affordably ($5–$20)
- Lower prices correlate with higher popularity

.5 Yearly Trends
- Some books appear across multiple years
- Indicates long-term popularity

 4.6 Author Analysis
- Certain authors dominate the bestseller list
- Strong author branding influences sales

 4.7 Reviews vs Ratings
- Highly reviewed books tend to maintain high ratings
- Popularity often aligns with quality

---

 5. Key Findings

1. Non-Fiction books dominate the market  
2. High ratings (>4.5) are common among bestsellers  
3. Affordable pricing boosts popularity  
4. Some books sustain success over multiple years  
5. Established authors outperform new ones  
6. Reviews strongly indicate popularity  

---

 6. Business Implications

- Focus on Non-Fiction genres  
- Maintain competitive pricing  
- Invest in author branding  
- Encourage reviews and engagement  
- Promote long-term bestsellers  

---

7. Conclusion

Amazon bestsellers follow clear patterns driven by:
- High ratings  
- Strong visibility  
- Affordable pricing  
- Established authors  

Success is largely strategic rather than random.

---

8. Recommendations

- Prioritize quality and visibility  
- Build early audience trust  
- Keep pricing competitive  
- Promote high-review books
